result.name: #1: CPU utilization
result.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The average, over the last minute, of the percentage of time that this processor was not idle. Implementations may approximate this one minute smoothing period if necessary.

result.name: #2: CPU utilization
result.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The average, over the last minute, of the percentage of time that this processor was not idle. Implementations may approximate this one minute smoothing period if necessary.

result.name: #3: CPU utilization
result.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The average, over the last minute, of the percentage of time that this processor was not idle. Implementations may approximate this one minute smoothing period if necessary.

result.name: #4: CPU utilization
result.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The average, over the last minute, of the percentage of time that this processor was not idle. Implementations may approximate this one minute smoothing period if necessary.

result.name: ARP Permanentes RJ - Lista
result.params: :foreach a in=[/ip arp print as-value] do={:local address [:tostr ($a->"address")]; :local mac [:tostr ($a->"mac-address")]; :local interface [:tostr ($a->"interface")]; :local comment [:tostr ($a->"comment")]; :local dynamic [:tostr ($a->"dynamic")]; :if ((([:len $address] > 0) && ([:len $mac] > 0)) && (($dynamic = "false") || ($dynamic = "no") || ($dynamic = ""))) do={:put ($address . "|" . $mac . "|" . $interface . "|" . $comment)}}

result.name: Bridge Hosts RJ - Lista
result.params: :foreach b in=[/interface bridge host print as-value] do={:local mac [:tostr ($b->"mac-address")]; :local int [:tostr ($b->"on-interface")]; :local bridge [:tostr ($b->"bridge")]; :local local [:tostr ($b->"local")]; :if ([:len $mac] > 0) do={:put ($mac . "|" . $int . "|" . $bridge . "|" . $local)}}

result.name: DDNS RJ Status
result.params: :local host "monitgr2085.dyndns.org"; :local currentIP "189.59.235.43"; :local dns1 ""; :local dns2 ""; :local dns3 ""; :local disabled "missing"; :do {:set dns1 [:tostr [:resolve $host server=8.8.8.8]]} on-error={}; :do {:set dns2 [:tostr [:resolve $host server=1.1.1.1]]} on-error={}; :do {:set dns3 [:tostr [:resolve $host server=9.9.9.9]]} on-error={}; :local sch [/system scheduler find where name="dyndns-rj-auto"]; :if ([:len $sch] > 0) do={:set disabled [:tostr [/system scheduler get [:pick $sch 0] disabled]]}; :put ("DDNS_RJ|" . $host . "|" . $currentIP . "|" . $dns1 . "|" . $dns2 . "|" . $dns3 . "|" . $disabled)

result.name: DHCP Leases RJ - Lista
result.params: :foreach l in=[/ip dhcp-server lease print as-value] do={:local address [:tostr ($l->"address")]; :local mac [:tostr ($l->"mac-address")]; :local host [:tostr ($l->"host-name")]; :local status [:tostr ($l->"status")]; :local dynamic [:tostr ($l->"dynamic")]; :local expires [:tostr ($l->"expires-after")]; :local lastseen [:tostr ($l->"last-seen")]; :local comment [:tostr ($l->"comment")]; :if ([:len $address] > 0) do={:put ($address . "|" . $mac . "|" . $host . "|" . $status . "|" . $dynamic . "|" . $expires . "|" . $lastseen . "|" . $comment)}}

result.name: Device: Temperature
result.description:
MIB: MIKROTIK-MIB
mtxrHlTemperature Device temperature in Celsius (degrees C).
Might be missing in entry models (RB750, RB450G..).

Reference: http://wiki.mikrotik.com/wiki/Manual:SNMP

result.name: Disk-131072: Space utilization
result.description: The space utilization expressed in % for Disk-131072.

result.name: Disk-131072: Total space
result.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The size of the storage represented by this entry, in
units of hrStorageAllocationUnits. This object is
writable to allow remote configuration of the size of
the storage area in those cases where such an
operation makes sense and is possible on the
underlying system. For example, the amount of main
memory allocated to a buffer pool might be modified or
the amount of disk space allocated to virtual memory
might be modified.

result.name: Disk-131072: Used space
result.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of the storage represented by this entry that is allocated, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.

result.name: Eventos MikroTik RJ - Log Recente
result.params: :local logs [/log print as-value where topics~"interface|ipsec|error|warning|critical|route|dhcp|ppp|pppoe"]; :local total [:len $logs]; :local start 0; :if ($total > 60) do={:set start ($total - 60)}; :for i from=$start to=($total - 1) do={:do {:local l [:pick $logs $i]; :local t [:tostr ($l->"time")]; :local topics [:tostr ($l->"topics")]; :local msg [:tostr ($l->"message")]; :if (([:len $t] > 0) && ([:len $msg] > 0)) do={:put ($t . "|" . $topics . "|" . $msg)}} on-error={}}

result.name: Failover Internet RJ - Status
result.params: :local vivoRoute [:len [/ip route find where dst-address=0.0.0.0/0 routing-table=main gateway=189.59.235.42 active]]; :local webRoute [:len [/ip route find where dst-address=0.0.0.0/0 routing-table=main gateway=pppoe-webcentro active]]; :local vivo [/interface monitor-traffic ether1 once as-value]; :local web [/interface monitor-traffic ether2 once as-value]; :put ("LINK_VIVO|ether1|" . $vivoRoute . "|" . ($vivo->"rx-bits-per-second") . "|" . ($vivo->"tx-bits-per-second")); :put ("LINK_WEBCENTRO|ether2|" . $webRoute . "|" . ($web->"rx-bits-per-second") . "|" . ($web->"tx-bits-per-second"))

result.name: Firmware version
result.description:
MIB: MIKROTIK-MIB
Current firmware version.

result.name: Hardware serial number
result.description:
MIB: MIKROTIK-MIB
RouterBOARD serial number.

result.name: ICMP loss
result.description: The percentage of lost packets.

result.name: ICMP ping
ICMP response time
result.description: The ICMP ping response time (in seconds).

result.name: Interface Loopback(): Bits received
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets received on the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifInOctets. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

result.name: Interface Loopback(): Bits sent
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifOutOctets.Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

result.name: Interface Loopback(): Inbound packets discarded
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of inbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system,
and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

result.name: Interface Loopback(): Inbound packets with errors
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of inbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of inbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

result.name: Interface Loopback(): Interface type
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The type of interface.
Additional values for ifType are assigned by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA),
through updating the syntax of the IANAifType textual convention.

result.name: Interface Loopback(): Operational status
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interface is ready to transmit and receive network traffic
- It should change todormant(5) if the interface is waiting for external actions (such as a serial line waiting for an incoming connection)
- It should remain in the down(2) state if and only if there is a fault that prevents it from going to the up(1) state
- It should remain in the notPresent(6) state if the interface has missing(typically, hardware) components.

result.name: Interface Loopback(): Outbound packets discarded
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of outbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system,
and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

result.name: Interface Loopback(): Outbound packets with errors
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of outbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of outbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

result.name: Interface Loopback(): Speed
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
An estimate of the interface's current bandwidth in units of 1,000,000 bits per second. If this object reports a value of `n`, then the speed of the interface is somewhere in the range of `n-500,000` to `n+499,999`.
For interfaces which do not vary in bandwidth or for those where no accurate estimation can be made, this object should contain the nominal bandwidth.
For a sub-layer which has no concept of bandwidth, this object should be zero.

result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of inbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of inbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

result.name: Interface ether4(DVR ================================================): Interface type
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The type of interface.
Additional values for ifType are assigned by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA),
through updating the syntax of the IANAifType textual convention.

result.name: Interface ether4(DVR ================================================): Operational status
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interface is ready to transmit and receive network traffic
- It should change todormant(5) if the interface is waiting for external actions (such as a serial line waiting for an incoming connection)
- It should remain in the down(2) state if and only if there is a fault that prevents it from going to the up(1) state
- It should remain in the notPresent(6) state if the interface has missing(typically, hardware) components.

result.name: Interface ether4(DVR ================================================): Outbound packets discarded
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of outbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system,
and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

result.name: Interface ether4(DVR ================================================): Outbound packets with errors
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of outbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of outbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

result.name: Interface ether4(DVR ================================================): Speed
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
An estimate of the interface's current bandwidth in units of 1,000,000 bits per second. If this object reports a value of `n`, then the speed of the interface is somewhere in the range of `n-500,000` to `n+499,999`.
For interfaces which do not vary in bandwidth or for those where no accurate estimation can be made, this object should contain the nominal bandwidth.
For a sub-layer which has no concept of bandwidth, this object should be zero.

result.name: Interface ether5(SWITCH =============================================): Bits received
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets received on the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifInOctets. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

result.name: Interface ether5(SWITCH =============================================): Bits sent
result.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifOutOctets.Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

result.name: Interface ether5(SWITCH =============================================): Inbound packets discarded

result.name: Links Internet RJ Trafego Atual
result.params: :local vivoPhys [/interface ethernet get ether1 running]; :local webPhys [/interface ethernet get ether2 running]; :local pppoeRun false; :local pppoeDisabled true; :do {:set pppoeRun [/interface get pppoe-webcentro running]; :set pppoeDisabled [/interface get pppoe-webcentro disabled]} on-error={}; :local vivoRoute [:len [/ip route find where dst-address=0.0.0.0/0 gateway=189.59.235.42 active]]; :local webRoute [:len [/ip route find where dst-address=0.0.0.0/0 gateway=pppoe-webcentro active]]; :local vivo [/interface monitor-traffic ether1 once as-value]; :local web [/interface monitor-traffic ether2 once as-value]; :put ("LINK_VIVO|ether1|" . $vivoPhys . "|" . $vivoRoute . "|" . ($vivo->"rx-bits-per-second") . "|" . ($vivo->"tx-bits-per-second")); :put ("LINK_WEBCENTRO|ether2|" . $webPhys . "|" . $pppoeRun . "|" . $webRoute . "|" . ($web->"rx-bits-per-second") . "|" . ($web->"tx-bits-per-second"))

result.name: Mangle Internet RJ Status
result.params: :local vivo [/ip firewall mangle find where new-routing-mark=to_vivo]; :local web [/ip firewall mangle find where new-routing-mark=to_webcentro]; :if ([:len $vivo] > 0) do={:local r [:pick $vivo 0]; :put ("MANGLE_DVR_VIVO|to_vivo|" . [/ip firewall mangle get $r disabled] . "|" . [/ip firewall mangle get $r bytes] . "|" . [/ip firewall mangle get $r packets])} else={:put "MANGLE_DVR_VIVO|to_vivo|missing|0|0"}; :if ([:len $web] > 0) do={:local r [:pick $web 0]; :put ("MANGLE_USUARIOS_WEBCENTRO|to_webcentro|" . [/ip firewall mangle get $r disabled] . "|" . [/ip firewall mangle get $r bytes] . "|" . [/ip firewall mangle get $r packets])} else={:put "MANGLE_USUARIOS_WEBCENTRO|to_webcentro|missing|0|0"}

result.name: Memory utilization
result.description: Memory utilization in %.

result.name: Mikrotik: SNMP walk network interfaces
result.description: Discovering interfaces from IF-MIB.

result.name: Operating system
result.description:
MIB: MIKROTIK-MIB
Software version.

result.name: SNMP agent availability
result.description:
Availability of SNMP checks on the host. The value of this item corresponds to availability icons in the host list.
Possible values:
0 - not available
1 - available
2 - unknown

result.name: SNMP traps (fallback)
result.description: The item is used to collect all SNMP traps unmatched by other snmptrap items

result.name: SNMP walk mounted filesystems
result.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
Discovering mounted filesystems.

result.name: SNMP walk system CPUs
result.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
Discovering system CPUs.

result.name: System contact details
result.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The textual identification of the contact person for this managed node, together with information on how to contact this person. If no contact information is known, the value is the zero-length string.

result.name: System description
result.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
A textual description of the entity. This value should
include the full name and version identification of the system's hardware type, software operating-system, and
networking software.

result.name: System location
result.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Physical location of the node (e.g., `equipment room`, `3rd floor`). If not provided, the value is a zero-length string.

result.name: System name
result.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
An administratively-assigned name for this managed node.By convention, this is the node's fully-qualified domain name. If the name is unknown, the value is the zero-length string.

result.name: System object ID
result.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The vendor's authoritative identification of the network management subsystem contained in the entity. This value is allocated within the SMI enterprises subtree (1.3.6.1.4.1) and provides an easy and unambiguous means for determining`what kind of box' is being managed.  For example, if vendor`Flintstones, Inc.' was assigned the subtree1.3.6.1.4.1.4242, it could assign the identifier 1.3.6.1.4.1.4242.1.1 to its `Fred Router'.

result.name: Top Destinos RJ - Conexões Ativas
result.params: :foreach c in=[/ip firewall connection print as-value where src-address~"^192\\.168\\.21\\."] do={:do {:local src [:tostr ($c->"src-address")]; :local dst [:tostr ($c->"dst-address")]; :local dport [:tostr ($c->"dst-port")]; :local proto [:tostr ($c->"protocol")]; :local orig [:tostr ($c->"orig-bytes")]; :local repl [:tostr ($c->"repl-bytes")]; :local orate [:tostr ($c->"orig-rate")]; :local rrate [:tostr ($c->"repl-rate")]; :if ($orig = "") do={:set orig "0"}; :if ($repl = "") do={:set repl "0"}; :if ($orate = "") do={:set orate "0"}; :if ($rrate = "") do={:set rrate "0"}; :if (([:len $src] > 0) && ([:len $dst] > 0) && ([:len $dport] > 0) && ([:len $proto] > 0)) do={:put ($src . "|" . $dst . "|" . $dport . "|" . $proto . "|" . $orig . "|" . $repl . "|" . $orate . "|" . $rrate)}} on-error={}}

result.name: Total memory
result.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The size of the storage represented by this entry, in
units of hrStorageAllocationUnits. This object is
writable to allow remote configuration of the size of
the storage area in those cases where such an
operation makes sense and is possible on the
underlying system. For example, the amount of main
memory allocated to a buffer pool might be modified or
the amount of disk space allocated to virtual memory
might be modified.

result.name: Uptime (hardware)
result.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of time since this host was last initialized. Note that this is different from sysUpTime in the SNMPv2-MIB [RFC1907] because sysUpTime is the uptime of the network management portion of the system.

result.name: Uptime (network)
result.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Time (in hundredths of a second) since the network management portion of the system was last re-initialized.

result.name: Used memory
result.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of the storage represented by this entry that is allocated, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.

result.name: VPN IPsec RJ-SP - Phase 1
result.params: :do {/ip ipsec active-peers print count-only where remote-address=189.112.96.221 state=established} on-error={:log warning "ZBX_ERR:VPN_PHASE1"}

result.name: VPN IPsec RJ-SP - Phase 2
result.params: :do {/ip ipsec policy print count-only where peer=peer2 ph2-state=established} on-error={:log warning "ZBX_ERR:VPN_PHASE2"}

result.name: VPN Matriz RJ - Conexões Ativas
result.params: :foreach c in=[/ip firewall connection print as-value where src-address~"^192\\.168\\.21\\." dst-address~"^172\\.20\\."] do={:do {:local src ""; :local dst ""; :local dport ""; :local proto ""; :do {:set src [:tostr ($c->"src-address")]} on-error={:set src ""}; :do {:set dst [:tostr ($c->"dst-address")]} on-error={:set dst ""}; :do {:set dport [:tostr ($c->"dst-port")]} on-error={:set dport ""}; :do {:set proto [:tostr ($c->"protocol")]} on-error={:set proto ""}; :if (([:len $src] > 0) && ([:len $dst] > 0) && ([:len $proto] > 0)) do={:put ($src . "|" . $dst . "|" . $dport . "|" . $proto)}} on-error={:log warning "ZBX_ERR:VPN_CONEXOES"}}

result.name: VPN Site-to-Site RJ-SP - Ping LAN RJ
